title: Shipyard repo config
description: Schema for shipyard.config.json (committed, repo-level) and .shipyard/config.local.json (gitignored, personal override). The latter is sparse — only fields the user wants to override locally.

$schema.description: Optional schema reference for editor autocomplete.

version.description: Schema version. Increment on breaking changes. Additive changes within a major version don't bump this.

policy.description: always = arm auto-merge on every PR; trusted-only = arm only when originating_author_trust == trusted (default); never = never arm auto-merge.

authors.description: GitHub usernames whose issues are dispatch-eligible without external-author gating. Overrides the user-global trusted-authors list (does not merge — repo override is authoritative).

default.description: Default size of the /do-work rolling worker pool when no --concurrency CLI flag is passed. Defaults to 1 (sequential) — most repos bump a manifest (plugin.json) or version row (CHANGELOG.md) on every PR, which the dispatch rules treat as a HARD path collision, parking every second-slot candidate. Set to 2 or higher only on repos where most PRs don't claim a shared manifest/version path and realized parallelism is real. See issue #268 for the dogfooding rationale.

soft_collision_paths.description: Per-repo extension of the built-in soft-collision glob set. The orchestrator unions this array with the built-in default set (defined in commands/do-work/steady-state.md's Dispatch rules) AND any --soft-collision-path CLI flags at dispatch time. Arrays here are additive, not replacing — the layered merge of config (last-wins) still applies, but the *consumer* (orchestrator) does the three-way union. Globs follow bash globstar semantics: `**` matches across path segments, `*` matches within a segment. See issue #254 for the dogfooding rationale (shipyard's own do-work spec files force effective concurrency=1 without this extension).

main_ci.description: Controls which workflows on the default branch make main 'red' for purposes of enqueueing a fix-main-ci divert (step 4.5a). See issue #262 — by default, only branch-protection-required workflows gate; non-required infra/release helpers still surface in the status line as informational but never trigger a divert.

aggregation_mode.description: How to scope the gating set. 'branch-protection' (default) reads required_status_checks from the default branch's protection rule and gates on those workflows only. 'all-workflows' restores pre-#262 behavior — every workflow gates, no branch-protection probe. Setting `required_workflows` to a non-empty array overrides this and acts as an explicit list.

required_workflows.description: Explicit list of workflow names that gate main_ci.status red. Highest priority — when non-empty, overrides `aggregation_mode` and the branch-protection probe. Match is case-sensitive against `workflowName` from `gh run list`. Empty array (the default) defers to `aggregation_mode`.

$comment: Forbidden field surfaces: anything matching /token|secret|api_key|password|credential/i is rejected at load time by shipyard-config.sh — the committed config must never carry secrets. Personal overrides in .shipyard/config.local.json are gitignored but the same rule applies (helps catch accidental paste into the wrong file).
